danger_signals.meaning: Nearby enemies (Aliens/Facehuggers) detected; immediate threat proximity

danger_signals.visual_form: Large Alien character models (noted as larger on Genesis version)
danger_signals.meaning: Direct combat threat; xenomorph hostility in area
Parasitic threat; infection/impregnation risk in Alien lore context

danger_signals.visual_form: On-screen timer countdown for level objectives
danger_signals.meaning: Resource scarcity; consequence of mission failure via time expiration

danger_signals.visual_form: Closed doorways blocking passage (objective: seal to prevent Alien entry)
danger_signals.meaning: Containment failure; Aliens breaching sealed sections

safety_signals.visual_form: Interactive terminal nodes placed throughout prison layout
safety_signals.meaning: Mission briefing/objective selection point; safe interaction zone

safety_signals.visual_form: Prison layout maps accessible from terminals
safety_signals.meaning: Environmental knowledge; strategic navigation advantage in hostile facility

safety_signals.visual_form: Pulse rifles, flamethrowers, grenades as usable equipment
safety_signals.meaning: Offensive capability; tool for threat mitigation

safety_signals.visual_form: Prisoner locations marked as level objectives
safety_signals.meaning: Non-hostile human presence; cooperative survival narrative

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Fiorina 161 prison colony insignia (inferred from setting)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Computer terminals, prison infrastructure, rescue-able prisoners

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Alien (standard form), Facehugger (parasitic form), Alien Queen (boss form)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Xenomorph-infested sections of prison; sealed contamination zones

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Alien and Facehugger entities present in level spaces; motion tracker signals
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies patrol/attack; level timer enforces urgency of containment/escape

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Sealed doors as objective targets; breached sections accessible to player
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must actively seal doors to prevent Alien spread; rescue missions indicate collapsed order

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Large arsenal available (pulse rifles, flamethrowers, grenades); gray/metallic industrial aesthetic
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Single-player progression through facility; combat-focused level design

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Alien Queen boss battle as final level objective
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Climactic confrontation; narrative resolution through direct combat

environmental_storytelling.example: Fiorina 161 prison colony layout communicated via blueprints; sealed sections and elevator systems reinforce setting authenticity
Mission types (seal doors vs. rescue prisoners) demonstrate dual survival challenges: containment and protection of remaining humans
Gray/metallic visual design across all versions reinforces industrial prison setting; atmosphere of confinement and technological isolation
Aliens and Facehuggers in specific level sections indicate infection spread; motion tracker use implies player must predict threat zones

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Detected by motion tracker; triggers combat engagement when player proximity detected
Distinct enemy type requiring weapon response; parasitic threat in Alien universe context
Boss-level entity; climactic battle mechanic signaling narrative culmination
Require player protection/retrieval; objective markers indicating non-hostile human presence
Continuous feedback on enemy proximity; tactical information source for player awareness

notes: Alien 3 Genesis manifests world-state primarily through enemy presence/motion tracker alerts and objective-based environmental interaction. The game communicates danger through alien entity visuals and time pressure mechanics. Factions are implied rather than explicitly marked: human colony (Ripley, prisoners, terminals) vs. xenomorph infestation (Aliens, Facehuggers, Queen). Political/social collapse is suggested through rescue missions and containment objectives rather than direct faction signaling. The gray/metallic aesthetic reinforces institutional dystopia. Genesis version features larger Alien models compared to SNES version, intensifying visual threat perception. Blueprint system suggests strategic intelligence gathering within hostile environment.
